title: Air-fryer scampi
subtitle: Crisp breaded prawns without deep-frying
excerpt: Large prawns coated in seasoned flour, egg, and panko breadcrumbs, and cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 8 to 10 minutes. The panko coating is crispier and lighter than the battered coating on pub scampi and the prawns inside stay moist rather than steaming in a thick batter shell.
sourceNotes: Scampi in British cooking refers to breaded or battered Norway lobster (langoustine) tails, a dish popularised through pub and chip-shop menus from the 1960s. Most commercial scampi now uses reformed fish or king prawns rather than langoustine. This recipe uses king prawns, which are widely available and produce a clean, recognisable result. Langoustine tails are a direct substitute for a more authentic version.

content.text: Press the panko firmly onto each prawn rather than tossing them in the breadcrumbs. The curved shape of a prawn means tossing leaves gaps in the coating. Pressing the breadcrumbs on by hand produces an even layer that stays on during cooking and crisps uniformly.

items.name: Raw prawns
items.prepNote: king prawns, peeled and deveined, tails on or off

items.name: Plain flour
items.prepNote: seasoned with salt, pepper, and a pinch of cayenne

content.text: Pat the {{prawns-raw}} dry. Set up three shallow bowls: seasoned {{plain-flour}}, beaten {{eggs}}, and {{breadcrumbs-panko}}. Working with one prawn at a time, coat in flour (shake off the excess), dip in egg, then press firmly into the panko on both sides. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 2 minutes. Spray the basket with oil. Arrange the scampi in a single layer. Spray the tops generously with oil. Cook at 200°C for 8 minutes for smaller prawns or 10 minutes for large ones, turning once at the halfway mark and spraying the second side with oil. They are done when deep golden all over and the prawns are pink and opaque inside. Serve immediately with {{lemon}}.

items.fix: Panko requires oil to brown. Spray generously on both sides at each stage. A light mist is not enough: the breadcrumb surface needs a proper coating of oil to brown in the air fryer
items.symptom: Panko coating pale and not crisping

items.fix: Very small prawns cook in 5 to 6 minutes. Check by breaking one open at 5 minutes: the prawn should be opaque and just firm, not tight and shrunken
items.cause: Prawns too small for the 8-minute cook time
items.symptom: Prawns overcooked and rubbery inside a crisp coating

items.fix: The egg wash must touch every part of the floured prawn. Press the panko on firmly with your fingers rather than relying on the egg wash alone to bond it
items.cause: Egg wash incomplete, or pressed too lightly
items.symptom: Coating falling off in the basket when flipped

content.text: Air-fryer scampi is a pub-style dinner at home. Serve with chips, peas, and tartare sauce for the full experience, or with a dressed watercress salad for a lighter plate. Frozen scampi from the freezer section cooks in the same time and produces a decent result; homemade is noticeably better.
